question: Rogue Access Point
canonical_answer: A wireless point that has either been installed on a secure company network without explicit authorization from a local network administrator, or has been created to allow a cracker to conduct a man-in-the-middle attack.

appears_in.title: Comptia Exam Objectives Security+
appears_in.answer: Wireless access point that has been installed on a secure network without explicit authorization from a local network administrator, whether added by a well-meaning employee or by a malicious attacker.

appears_in.title: Comptia Sec Plus Objectives
appears_in.answer: Attacker access valuable data via bluetooth device - High security issue

appears_in.title: Comptia Security+ Study Guide
appears_in.answer: A wireless point that has either been installed on a secure company network without explicit authorization from a local network administrator, or has been created to allow a cracker to conduct a man-in-the-middle attack.
